title: Rich Savory Portuguese Biscuit Pastry
description: A buttery, crumbly pastry from Portugal, perfect for a snack or light lunch, made with strong white flour and a lacquer-glaze.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: strong white flour, such as bread flour or all-purpose flour with a high protein content

ingredients.name: grated Parmesan cheese
ingredients.notes: can be substituted with other hard cheeses, such as cheddar or Pecorino

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, butter, Parmesan cheese, eggs, salt, and black pepper until a dough forms.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Cut the dough into desired shapes, such as squares or triangles.
Place the shapes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and crispy.
While the pastry is baking, prepare the lacquer-glaze by whisking together the egg yolks and 1 tablespoon of water.
Once the pastry is done, brush the lacquer-glaze over the top of the pastry.
Return the pastry to the oven for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the glaze is set.
Remove the pastry from the oven and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
Serve the pastry warm, or let it cool completely and store it in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
Serve with a side of your favorite dip or spread, such as hummus or tapenade.
